title: Celtics, Neemias Queta reportedly agree to four-year extension
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Celtics center Neemias Queta signed a four-year, $56 million extension, extending his contract through the 2030-31 season.
Queta, drafted by the Kings in 2021, had 10.2 points, 8.4 rebounds, and 1.3 blocks over 76 games last season.
The extension follows trades and signings, including Jaylen Brown's move to the 76ers and Mitchell Robinson joining the Celtics.

title: Union Oyster House turns 200: Inside America’s oldest continuously operating restaurant
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: Union Oyster House, established in 1826, celebrates 200 years as America's oldest continuously operating restaurant.
The building, possibly constructed in 1716, was a hub of activity during the Revolutionary War era.
The restaurant serves 1,000 to 1,500 patrons daily, with clam chowder and oysters as popular menu items.

title: Boston’s biggest summer events collide over the next 8 days. Buckle up.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: The Boston Pops Fireworks Spectacular will take place on July 4 with performances by Chance the Rapper and others.
A World Cup quarterfinal is scheduled at Boston Stadium (Gillette Stadium) on July 9, causing expected traffic congestions.
Sail Boston’s Parade of Sail begins July 11, featuring over 60 tall ships in Boston Harbor as part of America 250 celebrations.

title: The presidents, celebrities, and famous regulars of Boston’s Union Oyster House
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: Union Oyster House, operating since 1826, hosted famous guests including six U.S. presidents and numerous celebrities.
Presidents like JFK, FDR, and Obama, and celebs such as Matt Damon and Meryl Streep, have visited the Boston landmark.
In 2003, artist Stan Kotzen created a painting depicting over 50 legendary guests of Union Oyster House.

title: N.H. State Police announce unexpected death of 19-year veteran
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sgt. Brian J. Ross, a 19-year New Hampshire State Police veteran, died unexpectedly at 41, state police announced.
Ross, of Greenland, NH, served in roles like Major Crime Unit and Collision Analysis and Reconstruction Unit commander.
A memorial service is scheduled July 7 at United Church of Christ, North Hampton; public viewing on July 6.

title: Jaylen Brown says Celtics showed ‘lack of respect’ after trade to Sixers
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown expressed disappointment over his trade to the 76ers, citing a lack of respect from the Celtics.
Celtics General Manager Brad Stevens has been criticized for his handling of trade conversations with Brown.
The Celtics traded Brown to the Sixers for Paul George and several draft picks, surprising many analysts.

title: Hull man dies after apparent drowning at Nantasket Beach, officials say
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 76-year-old Hull man, Robert Nolan, died after an apparent drowning at Nantasket Beach on Wednesday evening.
Authorities found Nolan unresponsive in the water and pronounced him dead at around 8:40 p.m.
The Plymouth County DA's office reports no foul play is suspected in Nolan's death.

title: Don Sweeney, Bruins’ retooling plans remain a work in progress
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins GM Don Sweeney traded goalie Joonas Korpisalo to the Rangers for cap relief, acquiring defenseman Will Borgen.
Boston signed defensemen Connor Clifton and re-signed Jordan Harris, focusing on defensive upgrades during NHL free agency.
The Bruins retain $5.3 million cap space, planning further roster adjustments, potentially trading to reduce excess skaters.

title: Parking will be free at state parks, beaches for Fourth of July weekend
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Parking fees waived at all Massachusetts state parks and beaches from July 3-5 for the 250th anniversary of the American Revolution.
Free parking is available on a first come, first served basis; popular sites may close if parking reaches capacity.
Some state beaches remain closed due to water quality issues following sewer main breaks in Haverhill.

title: What’s up with those online ads for gas-saving gadgets?
author: John Paul
bullets: Online ads for gas-saving gadgets are raising skepticism; claims often unverified and products are not adopted by manufacturers.
Run-flat tires have improved but still provide a harsher ride and are more costly compared to conventional tires.
The cost for timing belt replacement on a 2002 Honda Accord is estimated at $1,000 to $1,200, considered a good investment.
